rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy of argv data into a 256-byte buffer and described that input longer than the buffer causes a global-buffer-overflow. They explicitly tied it to attacker-controlled command-line input exceeding the destination.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy and ensured null termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying as required. The criterion mandates rejecting oversized argv data, not just truncating.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended eliminating unsafe string functions and using bounded, length-aware APIs (e.g., snprintf/strlcpy or std::string), which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path. This satisfies the root-cause/global hardening requirement.
